title: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.26 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.26 (Threat-led penetration testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.17: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.17' running on index itsm and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises)
spl:
| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv
| eval last_tlpt_date=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since_tlpt=round((now()-last_tlpt_date)/(86400*30), 0)
| eval next_due=if(months_since_tlpt >= 36, "OVERDUE", if(months_since_tlpt >= 30, "DUE_WITHIN_6_MONTHS", "ON_TRACK"))
| eval findings_remediated=if(isnotnull(total_findings) AND isnotnull(findings_closed), round(100*findings_closed/total_findings,1), 0)
| table scope, tester_organization, completion_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, total_findings, findings_closed, findings_remediated, critical_findings_open
| sort next_due
description: Article 26 requires financial entities identified by competent authorities to conduct TLPT at least every three years, following TIBER-EU methodology with qualified external testers. This use case tracks the TLPT lifecycle — from threat intelligence scoping through red team execution to remediation of findings — ensuring the advanced testing programme that DORA mandates for systemically important entities is completed and acted upon.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create `dora_tlpt_register.csv` with TLPT engagement records (scope, tester, completion date, finding counts); (2) track three-year cycle per Art. 26; (3) monitor finding remediation — critical findings should be remediated within 90 days; (4) validate tester qualifications per Art. 27; (5) coordinate pooled TLPT with entities sharing the same ICT provider where applicable; (6) report TLPT status to competent authority. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Table (TLPT status), Single value (months since last TLPT), Gauge (finding remediation %), Bar chart (open findings by severity). Key fields: `completion_date`, `critical_findings_open`, `findings_closed`, `findings_remediated`, `last_tlpt_date`, `months_since_tlpt`.
knownFalsePositives: Financial entity reporting noise from coordinated testing, scenario drills, and reporting-calendar migrations that are not an ICT resilience breach.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create `dora_tlpt_register.csv` with TLPT engagement records (scope, tester, completion date, finding counts); (2) track three-year cycle per Art. 26; (3) monitor finding remediation — critical findings should be remediated within 90 days; (4) validate tester qualifications per Art. 27; (5) coordinate pooled TLPT with entities sharing the same ICT provider where applicable; (6) report TLPT status to competent authority.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv
| eval last_tlpt_date=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since_tlpt=round((now()-last_tlpt_date)/(86400*30), 0)
| eval next_due=if(months_since_tlpt >= 36, "OVERDUE", if(months_since_tlpt >= 30, "DUE_WITHIN_6_MONTHS", "ON_TRACK"))
| eval findings_remediated=if(isnotnull(total_findings) AND isnotnull(findings_closed), round(100*findings_closed/total_findings,1), 0)
| table scope, tester_organization, completion_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, total_findings, findings_closed, findings_remediated, critical_findings_open
| sort next_due
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle (Art. 26)** — Article 26 requires financial entities identified by competent authorities to conduct TLPT at least every three years, following TIBER-EU methodology with qualified external testers. This use case tracks the TLPT lifecycle — from threat intelligence scoping through red team execution to remediation of findings — ensuring the advanced testing programme that DORA mandates for systemically important entities is completed and acted upon.

Documented **Data sources**: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **last_tlpt_date** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **months_since_tlpt** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **next_due** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **findings_remediated** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: completion_date, critical_findings_open, findings_closed, findings_remediated, last_tlpt_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, scope.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (TLPT status), Single value (months since last TLPT), Gauge (finding remediation %), Bar chart (open findings by severity).
grandmaExplanation: We stay on top of threat-led penetration testing (tlpt) lifecycle — so we can show we govern technology and resilience risk the way financial rules that apply to us require.
